content.claim: Early tracking via 6- and 8-year gimnázium selection at ages 10–12 is independently linked to major educational inequality, not merely a proxy for prior ability differences; the huge performance differences among academic, vocational secondary, and vocational tracks in Hungary are attributable to the early tracking mechanism itself and are related to students' family socioeconomic status.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Single research lineage rather than uncontested international consensus
Disentangles tracking from SES in models but remains one interpretive strand
Does not separately isolate the causal share of 6/8-year gimnázium versus the broader 3-track secondary system
Published 2016; evidence base primarily from early-to-mid 2000s PISA data
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
